reasoning_trace.what_data_shows: Two premium brands (Suta, Ritu Kumar) selling Chanderi silk straight kurtis at ₹1,450-2,200 with near-zero discount, strong ratings, and editorial placement. One product has limited sizes indicating stock pressure.
reasoning_trace.what_it_proves: Premium urban customers are willing to pay full price for Chanderi silk straight kurtis. The trend has genuine demand at high price points.
reasoning_trace.what_it_cannot_prove: That value-fashion customers at ₹699-899 will buy it. Premium demand does not guarantee mass appeal. Also, Nykaa's customer base is metro-centric and may not represent tier-2/3 shoppers.
reasoning_trace.relationship_to_others: Strongly positive signal contrasts with weak Meesho presence and poor internal POS performance. Suggests a premium-only trend that hasn't trickled down.

reasoning_trace.what_data_shows: Biba's Chanderi straight kurti at ₹899-949 with 15-20% discount, high reviews (1,240 on Myntra), and strong review velocity (85/month). Ranked #3 on Myntra.
reasoning_trace.what_it_proves: Organized mass retail demand exists at ₹899-949 with moderate discounting. The silhouette and fabric have broad appeal in the value-premium segment.
reasoning_trace.what_it_cannot_prove: That demand is sustainable without discount. The 15-20% discount may be driving sales. Also, Biba's brand equity may be inflating demand; unbranded versions may not perform similarly.
reasoning_trace.relationship_to_others: Aligns with Nykaa in showing demand for Chanderi, but at lower price and with discount. Contradicts internal POS which shows poor sell-through for a similar product.

reasoning_trace.what_data_shows: A cotton imitation Chanderi kurti at ₹349 with 1,800+ units sold, 45 resellers, 3.4 rating. Reseller growth is +8% MoM but not accelerating. Only 4 regions.
reasoning_trace.what_it_proves: Price-sensitive mass market (tier-2/3/4) is buying a Chanderi-look product at ₹349. The concept of 'Chanderi style' has some traction at low price points.
reasoning_trace.what_it_cannot_prove: That genuine Chanderi silk at ₹699-899 would sell. The Meesho product is cotton imitation, not silk. Also, low rating (3.4) suggests quality issues. Cannot prove demand for the actual fabric.
reasoning_trace.relationship_to_others: Weak signal compared to Nykaa/Myntra. Shows that mass market wants the look but at much lower price and with different fabric. Contradicts internal POS where premium fabric underperformed.

reasoning_trace.what_data_shows: Similar Chanderi silk A-line kurti (SS24) stocked in 84 stores, 38% sell-through vs 65% target, 22% margin vs 40% target, 35% markdown, 18% return rate. Worked only in metros (Mumbai, Bangalore). Buyer note: fabric too delicate for daily wear.
reasoning_trace.what_it_proves: Chanderi silk has a narrow addressable market within our store base. Metro-only viability. Our core customer finds it too delicate and returns due to wash durability.
reasoning_trace.what_it_cannot_prove: Whether a different price point (sub-₹599) or blended fabric would change the outcome. Also, the past SKU was A-line, not straight; silhouette difference may affect demand.
reasoning_trace.relationship_to_others: Directly contradicts the positive signals from Nykaa and Myntra. Our actual sales data shows underperformance, while external platforms show demand. This is a HIGH conflict.
reasoning_trace.internal_verdict: Underperformed. 38% sell-through vs 65% target. Only viable in metro stores. Not recommended for full rollout.

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Nykaa and Myntra show strong demand for Chanderi silk at premium to mid prices, but internal POS shows poor sell-through and high returns for a similar product. Meesho shows mass market wants the look but at much lower price and with imitation fabric. The core tension: external demand signals (especially premium) conflict with our actual customer behavior.
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: All sources agree that Chanderi has some demand, but at different price points and customer segments. Nykaa and Myntra converge on premium/mass-premium demand. Meesho and internal POS converge on the fact that genuine Chanderi silk may not suit the mass or value customer.

chanderi-straight.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: External platforms show strong Chanderi demand, but our internal POS shows poor sell-through and high returns for a similar product.

conflicts.title: External demand vs internal performance
conflicts.nykaa_says: Strong premium demand at ₹1,450-2,200 with zero discount
conflicts.myntra_says: Strong mass-premium demand at ₹899-949 with 15-20% discount
conflicts.meesho_says: Weak signal: cotton imitation at ₹349, not genuine silk
conflicts.internal_says: 38% sell-through, 18% return rate, only metro viable. Fabric too delicate for daily wear.
conflicts.buyer_question: Will our core customer accept Chanderi silk at ₹699-899 given past durability complaints? Or should we consider a blended fabric?
conflicts.proves: ✅ This conflict proves that Chanderi silk has strong demand in premium and mass-premium channels but may not suit our value-conscious customer base.
conflicts.cannot_prove: ⚠️ Cannot prove whether a different silhouette (straight vs A-line) or lower price point would change internal performance. Also cannot prove that external demand is sustainable without discount.

conflicts.title: Fabric authenticity vs price sensitivity
conflicts.nykaa_says: Genuine Chanderi silk at ₹1,450+ sells well
conflicts.myntra_says: Genuine Chanderi silk at ₹899-949 sells with discount
conflicts.meesho_says: Cotton imitation at ₹349 sells, but rating is low (3.4)
conflicts.internal_says: Genuine Chanderi silk underperformed; returns cite wash durability
conflicts.buyer_question: Should we use genuine Chanderi silk or a blended fabric (e.g., cotton-silk) to balance premium look with durability?
conflicts.proves: ✅ This conflict proves that there is a market for both genuine and imitation Chanderi, but at different price points and with different quality expectations.
conflicts.cannot_prove: ⚠️ Cannot prove that a blended fabric would satisfy both premium look seekers and durability-conscious customers. Also cannot prove the optimal price for a blended version.

convergences.title: Chanderi style has broad appeal across segments
convergences.detail: Nykaa, Myntra, and Meesho all show some form of Chanderi-related product selling, indicating the aesthetic is popular from premium to mass market.
convergences.proves: ✅ This convergence proves that the Chanderi look is in demand across price tiers.
convergences.cannot_prove: ⚠️ Cannot prove that genuine Chanderi silk is the right fabric for our price band. The mass market prefers imitation at lower price.

convergences.title: Discounting is present in mass retail
convergences.detail: Myntra/Ajio show 15-20% discount on Chanderi kurtis, suggesting that even organized retail needs to discount to drive volume.
convergences.proves: ✅ This convergence proves that full-price demand for Chanderi is limited to premium segments.
convergences.cannot_prove: ⚠️ Cannot prove the exact discount depth needed for our price band or whether a no-discount strategy would work.

chanderi-straight.upside_summary: The Chanderi silk straight kurti has strong external demand signals from Nykaa (premium, full-price) and Myntra (mass-premium, discounted). If we can address the durability concerns (e.g., blended fabric), there is potential to capture a trend that is clearly resonating with urban and metro customers. However, this evidence cannot prove that our value-conscious base will accept even a blended version at ₹699-899.
chanderi-straight.catch_summary: Our internal POS data is a major red flag: a similar Chanderi silk product underperformed with 38% sell-through, high returns, and only metro viability. The fabric was deemed too delicate for daily wear by our customers. External demand may be driven by different customer profiles. This evidence cannot prove that a straight silhouette or lower price would overcome the durability issue.

nykaa.reason: Recent data (June 7), multiple products with strong reviews and editorial placement. Reliable premium demand signal.

myntra.reason: Recent data (June 5), high review count and velocity. Biba is a known brand. Discount level is moderate.

meesho.reason: Data from June 8, but product is imitation, not genuine silk. Low rating and non-accelerating growth limit reliability.

internal.reason: Direct match from SS24 buy with detailed sell-through, margin, and return data. Highly relevant ground truth.

chanderi-straight.bet_rationale: External demand signals are strong, but internal POS data shows a clear failure for a similar product. The conflict is HIGH. Recommend a small trial in metro stores (where internal data showed some success) with a blended fabric to test durability perception. Do not commit to full rollout.
chanderi-straight.key_uncertainty: Will a blended fabric (e.g., cotton-silk) at ₹699-899 overcome the durability complaints and achieve target sell-through?
watch_triggers: Meesho reseller growth for Chanderi-look products accelerates above +15% MoM
Internal POS trial of blended fabric shows sell-through above 50% in metro stores
Myntra discount depth on Chanderi kurtis increases above 25% (sign of demand softening)
Return rate on trial batch stays below 10%
missing_evidence: Consumer survey data on fabric preference (genuine silk vs blend) at ₹699-899
Competitor sell-through data for Chanderi straight kurtis in similar store formats
Wash durability test results for blended Chanderi fabric

reasoning_trace.signal: No products found; absent from premium
Strong presence but high discount risk

reasoning_trace.tension: Price gap vs Myntra suggests price sensitivity

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Internal past success vs external discount distortion
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: All sources confirm block print cotton A-line is a staple

blockprint-cotton.headline: CONFLICT: Internal past success vs external discount-driven demand; proceed with caution on margin.

conflicts.title: Internal past success vs external discount distortion
conflicts.buyer_question: Will our full-price offering (₹399-599) sustain demand without deep discounts?
conflicts.proves: Block print cotton A-line has proven demand at various price points
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether demand at our price band is organic or discount-dependent

convergences.title: Block print cotton A-line is a staple
convergences.detail: All sources confirm strong demand across price tiers (₹249-599). Internal past success (78% sell-through) aligns with Meesho volume (15k+ units) and Myntra presence.

blockprint-cotton.upside_summary: Strong internal track record (78% sell-through, 44% margin) and Meesho volume (15k+ units) confirm block print cotton A-line is a consistent winner. Under-ordering last time (stockouts in 45 stores) suggests unmet demand.
blockprint-cotton.catch_summary: Myntra/Ajio discounts (10-30%) may condition customers to expect deals, potentially pressuring our margins. Meesho's ₹249 price point highlights extreme price sensitivity in lower tiers. Design fatigue risk from repeated block print styles.

nykaa.reason: No products found; irrelevant for this price band

myntra.reason: High discount distortion risk; reviews moderate

meesho.reason: Strong volume and reseller growth; price gap noted

internal.reason: Ground truth from 210 stores; high sell-through and margin

blockprint-cotton.bet_rationale: Internal data proves this is a core category with strong past performance. However, external discounting and price sensitivity warrant a cautious approach to avoid margin erosion.
blockprint-cotton.key_uncertainty: Will current block print designs achieve similar sell-through without deep discounts?
watch_triggers: Internal early sell-through below 60% in first 2 weeks
